description: Fixed Cat15 (Polymarket) zero-variance fallback. polymarket_home_prob and prediction_market_consensus previously defaulted to constant 0.5 for all historical games (polymarket data never available during training). Now fall back to book-implied probability from odds_data: implied_home_prob for polymarket_home_prob, fair_home_prob for prediction_market_consensus. This converts 2 of 8 zero-variance Cat15 features into features with real variance, allowing them to survive the zero-variance filter in app.py and contribute genuine signal to the model. Feature names unchanged; only fallback values improved.
root_cause: Cat15 polymarket features fell back to constant 0.5 (or 0.0) for ALL historical games because polymarket data is never in market_data during training. The zero-variance filter in app.py (variances > 1e-10) correctly removed them, but this wasted 8 feature name slots. The fix uses odds_data — already loaded from CSV via load_historical_odds() — to provide book-implied probabilities as fallbacks for polymarket_home_prob and prediction_market_consensus. The odds_data dict is keyed by (gd, home, away) tuples, same pattern as Cat46, so the lookup is consistent and correct.

test_suite.note: No feature names or count changed. Only fallback values for polymarket_home_prob and prediction_market_consensus improved from constant 0.5 to book-implied probability. Zero structural regressions expected. When odds_data has no entry for a game (early season or missing CSV data), fallback remains 0.5 — identical to prior behavior.

audit_findings.detail: 4 bare except: blocks in odds parsing (lines 271, 276, 284, 288) — FIXED iter 9
9 bare except: blocks in datetime helpers — FIXED iter 11
1 bare except: in CV model fitting (line 7060) — FIXED iter 12
ats53_a_away_only was silently computing all-game ATS rate due to `if home_only:` treating False as falsy — FIXED iter 13
_season_spreads/_season_totals accumulated cross-season, making Cat52 percentile/std features lifetime metrics instead of within-season — FIXED iter 14
Cat52 fallback row line52_spread_agree=1.0 biased all no-odds rows toward home-agree direction — FIXED iter 15
Cat14 defensive matchup: shot_contest_rate=0.6 and rim_protection_rate=0.55 were hardcoded constants providing zero team-differentiation signal and positive directional bias — FIXED iter 16

Cat15 polymarket: polymarket_home_prob=0.5 and prediction_market_consensus=0.5 were constant defaults (polymarket data never available during training), creating zero-variance features that were filtered out by app.py zero-variance filter — FIXED iter 17

next_iteration.method: Cat15 still has 6 features with potential zero-variance when both polymarket AND odds_data are unavailable: polymarket_volume (0.5), polymarket_line_movement (0.0), polymarket_vs_books (0.0), market_wisdom_confidence (0.5), smart_vs_public_divergence (0.0), closing_line_value_history (0.0). Check: (a) whether odds_data provides proxies for these (e.g., books_disagreement for market_wisdom_confidence, smart_money_indicator for smart_vs_public_divergence, clv_recent_avg for closing_line_value_history — all already in Cat9 mkt dict but require market_data not odds_data), (b) audit Cat16-20 interaction features for any pairs that both reference zero-variance source features (creating zero-variance products).
